title: Not storing signatures locally
body:
Hi, I'm running Ubuntu on WSL, using skopeo version 1.2.2 and have the following configuration in my `/etc/containers/registries.d/default.yaml`:

```
# This is a default registries.d configuration file.  You may
# add to this file or create additional files in registries.d/.
#
# sigstore: indicates a location that is read and write
# sigstore-staging: indicates a location that is only for write
#
# sigstore and sigstore-staging take a value of the following:
#   sigstore:  {schema}://location
#
# For reading signatures, schema may be http, https, or file.
# For writing signatures, schema may only be file.

# This is the default signature write location for docker registries.
default-docker:
#  sigstore: file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
  sigstore-staging: file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore

# The 'docker' indicator here is the start of the configuration
# for docker registries.
#
docker:
  uk.icr.io/test/testimage:1:
    sigstore: file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
    sigstore-staging: file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore
```

I am using IBM Cloud Container Registry (ICCR) which according to [here](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/Registry?topic=Registry-registry_overview#docker):

>IBM Cloud Container Registry supports Docker Engine V1.12.6, or later.
>
>Docker is required only if you want to push or pull images, or you want to run the ibmcloud cr ppa-archive-load command.
>
> Docker V2 Schema 2 images are supported. Manifest lists are also supported. For more information, see Docker: Image Manifest Version 2, Schema 2.

When I run `sudo skopeo copy --sign-by none@fake.com docker://uk.icr.io/test/testimage:1 docker://uk.icr.io/test/testimage:1-signed --debug`, I see:

```
Writing manifest to image destination
DEBU[0005] PUT https://uk.icr.io/v2/test/testimage/manifests/1-signed
Signing manifest
Storing signatures
DEBU[0006] GET https://uk.icr.io/extensions/v2/test/testimage/signatures/sha256:4188f0016aa4cd08beb5823d45ade41ff90764eff6fbd89f1c6eb94002ce8ff5
DEBU[0006] PUT https://uk.icr.io/extensions/v2/test/testimage/signatures/sha256:4188f0016aa4cd08beb5823d45ade41ff90764eff6fbd89f1c6eb94002ce8ff5
```

And I cannot get it to place the image signature in `file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore`. However when I run this against an image on docker, it will store the signature in `file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore`.

Am I doing something wrong here, or misunderstanding how things work? I want to be able to copy the signature from `file:///var/lib/containers/sigstore` to a webserver at some point.

Thanks.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The registry declares support for on-registry signature storage, so the signatures should have been stored directly into the registry:
> ```
> DEBU[0006] PUT https://uk.icr.io/extensions/v2/test/testimage/signatures/sha256:4188f0016aa4cd08beb5823d45ade41ff90764eff6fbd89f1c6eb94002ce8ff5
> ```

Similarly, reading images from that registry should access the signatures directly from the registry. So you shouldn’t need to (and, indeed, with recent implementations, can’t) use the separate `sigstore` web server with this registry.
